MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-03-2021 Set For Case Management Conference On Jan-12-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Amended MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - Amended Re-Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-03-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KENDRA LEINGANG's Amended MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Kendra Leingang's summary judgment motion is granted.

Thomas Stefan's complaint pled one cause of action: breach of written contract. Neither the complaint nor Stefan's opposition brief clearly identifies the supposed written contract, and, asked to identify it in an interrogatory, he stated: "No written documentation that I know of." (Robinson Dec. Ex. F pp.18-19.) Stefan's separate statement asserts: "The agreement was to have intensive twice weekly therapy session 'to get me back on my horse.'" (RSS 21.) However, Stefan cites no written agreement to that, or any, effect. (Id.)

Leingang seeks summary judgment on, inter alia, three contract-formation grounds: "(1) the terms of the alleged contract were never clear, (2) Plaintiff offered no consideration to Defendant, and (3) Defendant never accepted, or agreed to, the terms of the alleged contract" - all of which a plaintiff must prove. (CACI 302.)

Whether a contract is sufficiently definite is a question of law for the court. (Ladas v. California State Automobile Assn. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 770 n.2.) As shown above, the terms of the alleged contract were never clear; indeed, it is not clear what Stefan claims the written contract, if any, was. Nor did Stefan offer any consideration to Leingang. (RSS 11-13.) And Stefan makes no showing that Leingang agreed to the terms of an alleged contract.

Given the above findings, other issues need not be reached.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
